title: Do as I Do: Dexterous Manipulation Data from Everyday Human Videos
summary: This work introduces an algorithm that reconstructs hand-object interactions from egocentric and exocentric in-the-wild video and retargets these estimates into action sequences executable by multi-fingered dexterous robotic hands. It addresses two obstacles: accurately estimating hand-object interactions from monocular RGB footage, and bridging the embodiment gap between human and robotic platforms. Experiments show improvements over prior methods in interaction estimation and trajectory extraction, giving a scalable framework for collecting human manipulation data for robot training.
